title: The switch flicking off
subtitle: A short evening visualisation for helping the nervous system move from 'on' to 'off'.
excerpt: A five-minute visualisation from Day 18 of the SLEEP program. The scene: a light switch, the click, the dark, the body settling into rest.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Creative visualisation as a self-development tool draws on public-domain traditions.

content.text: A five-minute visualisation from the Day 18 theme. The image is simple: a switch, a click, and the safe quiet that follows.
Lie down. Close your eyes. One slow breath.
Picture a light switch on a wall. It is a standard switch: white plastic, slightly raised. The light in the room is on.

You reach up and flick the switch. Click. The room goes dark. Not frightening dark: the familiar dark of a room you know well. The shapes are still there. Everything is where it was. The house is quiet.
Now notice the body. The click of the switch is permission. The day's tasks are done. The night watch is handed over. There is nothing that requires you to be alert right now.
Feel the weight of the body into the mattress. The neck softening. The jaw. The hands. Each part receives the click of the switch: off, safe, done for tonight.
Stay with the dark and the quiet for two minutes. If the mind turns something on again, notice it and reach for the switch. Click. Dark. Safe.
Written for homemade.education. Draws on the long use of guided imagery as a calming practice.
